title: salix × sadleri
intro: Salix × sadleri, a hybrid of the weeping willow (Salix babylonica) and the goat willow (Salix caprea), is a deciduous shrub or small tree known for its slender branches and white or yellowish catkins. The bark and leaves of the Salix × sadleri plant contain salicin, a compound that is similar to aspirin, which has been used to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and lower fever. The medicinal properties of Salix × sadleri have been used to treat various health issues, including rheumatoid arthritis, headaches, and menstrual cramps, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or tincture, and its extracts are also used in topical creams and ointments to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
